title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Panamax Vessel Speed Ballast / Laden Speed and Freight Index
Upward revision to Panamax ship speed in August as freight market signals recover
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/amic-drybulkflows-downloadable/2p9d88/340887919?h=TBVl4pMEiX_MWhVTqtZnvCd3CoUmdGuPfMXPq7JcDDY
The third week of August began with more robust sentiment, which countered fears about a possible downward trend in the Capesize segment. This change in sentiment is particularly evident as the Baltic Dry Index gains momentum, driven by Panamax and smaller ship categories. The upward trend in Panamax freight prices has led to a corresponding acceleration in vessel speeds, both ballasted and loaded.

children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week: Panamax Vessel Speed Ballast / Laden Speed and Freight Index
children.summary:
Upward revision to Panamax ship speed in August as freight market signals recover
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-23T115648.998.png | assets/2023-08-23_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-23t115648998_8986f1ad10d6.png
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/amic-drybulkflows-downloadable/2p9d88/340887919?h=TBVl4pMEiX_MWhVTqtZnvCd3CoUmdGuPfMXPq7JcDDY
The third week of August began with more robust sentiment, which countered fears about a possible downward trend in the Capesize segment. This change in sentiment is particularly evident as the Baltic Dry Index gains momentum, driven by Panamax and smaller ship categories.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes Market Rates ($/t) Firmer
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-23T115648.998.png | assets/2023-08-23_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-23t115648998_8986f1ad10d6.png
The third week of August started with a firmer sentiment with the increase being more profound in the Panamax vessel segment, while a downward pressure was revealed in the Capesize segment. Capesize vessel freight rates from Brazil to North China fell below $19/tonne, while stronger momentum appeared to be in place at the end of the previous week.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas Ballasters (# vessels) Mixed
Image reference: assets/2023-08-23_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-23t115950351_091c226be05d.png
The number of ballast vessels showed a mixed picture with a downward trend in the Capesize and Panamax regions, although the Handy NOPAC region continues to experience upward pressure. .Capesize SE Africa: The number of ballasters is 115, 9% below the peak in week 26.

children.title: SECTION 3/ DEMAND
children.summary:
Summary of Dry Bulk Demand, per Ship Size TonneDays Decreasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-23T120116.410.png | assets/2023-08-23_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-23t120116410_37b140779961.png
The upward surge in tonne-day growth seen during the second week of August appears to have levelled off with a downward trend in the large vessel categories. Capesize: Demand for Capesize ships has slowed in recent days, with the last peak recorded in week 26.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION
children.summary:
Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports No of Vessels Decreasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-08-23T120323.129.png | assets/2023-08-23_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-08-23t120323129_94b76d4351cb.png
The upward revision in the number of vessel congestions appears to have moderated after the last peak in week 32, but the increase in the Panamax segment remained strong. Capesize: The current number of vessel congestion is 109, 10 lower than in week 32.
